name: Baumann Bus Co Inc
owner_or_parent: defunct (formerly Baumann family / A.B.A. Transportation affiliate)
recent_news: Baumann Bus Company (and affiliate Acme Bus) ceased operations in 2020 citing COVID-19 impact; all employees laid off. Company is defunct - NOT a viable tuck-in target.
fit_notes: DEFUNCT - company shut down in 2020 due to COVID-19. Not an active operator. Could potentially be route-purchase opportunity from successor district contracts on Long Island, but the entity itself is dead. Skip as tuck-in target.

name: Crete School Bus Svc
owner_or_parent: likely Crete Public Schools (in-house district operation)
recent_news: No evidence of independent contractor status. Address 920 Linden Ave, Crete NE matches Crete Public Schools Administration Office address - strongly suggests this is the district's in-house transportation department, not an independent private contractor. The 'Firstcharterbus.Com' website in source data does not appear to be the company's actual site. First Student acquired Mid States School Bus (Wayne, NE) but that is a separate company.
fit_notes: Likely a district-operated transportation department, not an acquirable private contractor. Tiny employee count (10) and shared address with Crete Public Schools admin office at 920 Linden Ave reinforces this. NOT a viable tuck-in target unless verified as independent.

name: We Care Transportation
owner_or_parent: Beacon Mobility (portfolio of Audax Private Equity)
recent_news: Rebranded from We Care Transportation to The Cedar Bus Company in November 2018. Acquired by Beacon Mobility (Audax PE portfolio company) in January 2024 - announced alongside Easton Bus Service (Leavenworth, KS). Foley & Lardner advised Beacon on the deal.
fit_notes: ALREADY OWNED BY A DIRECT COMPETITOR PLATFORM (Beacon Mobility / Audax). Not a tuck-in target. Note for competitive intelligence - this represents Beacon's WNY footprint via paratransit/special needs.

name: A Plus Meditrans
districts_or_customers: Albany, Columbia, Greene, Rensselaer, Saratoga, Schenectady, Schoharie counties
recent_news: Operating as A+ MediTrans, a Rejha Group company. Provides contracted school bus transportation for pre-school children under age 5 with identified developmental disabilities in the NY Capital Region. No M&A news identified.
fit_notes: Independent family-owned NEMT+SPED operator in NY Capital Region. Small (~18 employees, ~$1-5M revenue per ZoomInfo). Niche focus on pre-K developmental disability transport plus NEMT. Possible bolt-on for Summit's special-needs / SPED footprint in upstate NY, but small scale - more of a route/contract tuck-in than a platform addition.

name: Royal Silver Wheelchair
districts_or_customers: Plant City / Hillsborough County FL area
recent_news: Florida LLC formed April 27, 2020 (FEI/EIN 85-0912916). Active status with FL Department of State. No school district contracts identified - appears to be a small NEMT operator only.
fit_notes: Micro NEMT operator (3 employees, formed 2020). Not a school-bus contractor in any meaningful sense - pure non-emergency medical wheelchair transport. Too small and not aligned with Summit's school-bus platform thesis. Not a viable tuck-in.
